story.iWant: to describe a single slide I need in natural language
story.soThat: I can quickly get one slide without planning a full deck

tasks.task: Create plan-one Workflow Structure 1.1: Create `.slide-builder/workflows/plan-one/` directory 1.2: Create `workflow.yaml` with name, description, instructions path 1.3: Create `instructions.md` skeleton with workflow phases

Implement Theme Verification (Phase 1) 2.1: Add step to check for `.slide-builder/theme.json` existence 2.2: If theme missing, output error: "No theme found. Run /setup first." 2.3: If theme found, load theme for context and continue 2.4: Add unit test: verify error when theme.json missing

Implement Intent Capture (Phase 2) 3.1: Add prompt: "Describe the slide you need" 3.2: Capture user's natural language description 3.3: Parse description for key elements: purpose, content type, visual elements 3.4: Add unit test: verify intent parsed from various descriptions

Implement Template Matching (Phase 3) 4.1: Scan intent for template-matching keywords 4.2: Return template name if match found, "custom" if no match 4.3: Log decision in suggested_template field 4.4: Add unit test: verify keyword matching logic

Implement Confirmation (Phase 4) 5.1: Display understanding summary with slide purpose 5.2: Show key content elements extracted 5.3: Show suggested template (or "custom layout") 5.4: Ask: "Is this correct? (y/n)" 5.5: If no, return to Phase 2 for clarification 5.6: If yes, continue to save

Implement State Persistence (Phase 5) 6.1: Create `.slide-builder/single/` directory if not exists 6.2: Save plan.yaml with schema (created, intent, suggested_template, key_points, etc.) 6.3: Update status.yaml: mode: "single", last_action, history 6.4: Display: "Plan saved. Run /build-one to generate." 6.5: Add unit test: verify plan.yaml and status.yaml content
Register Slash Command 7.1: Add `/sb:plan-one` command registration in BMAD skill configuration 7.2: Verify command invokes plan-one workflow 7.3: Add integration test: full /plan-one workflow

Testing and Validation 8.1: Test with existing theme.json 8.2: Test with missing theme.json (error case) 8.3: Test various intent descriptions (architecture, list, flow, custom) 8.4: Verify plan.yaml schema matches tech spec 8.5: Verify status.yaml updates correctly 8.6: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a theme exists (theme.json), when the user runs `/plan-one`, then they are prompted to describe the slide they want

Given the user describes their slide (e.g., "An architecture diagram showing our 3-tier system"), when the description is submitted, then the system confirms understanding by summarizing: slide purpose, key content elements, suggested layout template (or "custom")
The intent is saved to `.slide-builder/single/plan.yaml` with all required fields (intent, suggested_template, key_points, created timestamp)
status.yaml is updated with mode: "single" and last_action recorded
Given no theme exists, when user runs `/plan-one`, then error message directs them to run `/setup` first

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Prompts user to describe their slide need in natural language. Parses description for key elements: purpose, content type, visual elements. Confirms understanding by summarizing back to user.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: 5-phase workflow: Theme Verification → Intent Capture → Template Matching → Confirmation → State Persistence. Clear error handling for missing theme.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Schema includes: created, last_modified, intent, suggested_template, audience, audience_knowledge_level, context, key_points, visual_guidance, tone, technical_depth, include_elements, exclude_elements.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Parse intent for content type keywords. Match against template patterns. If match found → use template. If no match → invoke frontend-design skill.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Mode detection based on plan.yaml existence. Single mode uses single/ directory, completely isolated from deck mode. Status.yaml tracks current mode.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Tracks mode (deck|single|null), current_slide, total_slides, built_count, last_action, last_modified timestamp, and history array.

doc.title: Slide Builder PRD
doc.snippet: FR12: Users can initiate single-slide planning via /plan-one. FR14: System confirms understanding and captures slide intent. FR15: Single-slide plan persists for building.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: /sb:plan-one maps to .slide-builder/workflows/plan-one/. All workflows follow BMAD pattern: Load workflow.yaml, read instructions.md, execute steps, update status.yaml.

file.reason: Existing workflow configuration - defines installed_path, instructions path, plan_file location (.slide-builder/single/plan.yaml), status_file. Foundation for implementation.
Existing instructions skeleton with 4-step workflow (verify theme, capture intent, confirm understanding, save plan). Needs expansion per tech spec phases.
Existing status tracking with mode, phase, assets, extraction, theme status, templates, last_action, history. Needs mode: "single" support added.
Theme primitives to verify exists before /plan-one runs. Contains colors, typography, shapes, layouts - used as context for intent matching.
Example template showing HTML slide structure with CSS variables, 1920x1080 viewport. Reference for template matching decision.

node.note: Not used in this story - HTML-to-image conversion for Epic 7
Not used in this story - Google Slides API for Epic 7

constraints.constraint: Theme.json MUST exist before /plan-one runs - check and error if missing
plan.yaml saved to .slide-builder/single/plan.yaml with all required fields
status.yaml updated with mode: "single", last_action, history entry
Template matching uses keyword scan: title/intro/opening → layout-title, list/bullets/points/agenda → layout-list, flow/process/timeline/steps → layout-flow, etc.
If no template keywords match, set suggested_template: "custom"
All state files (YAML/JSON) must be human-readable per NFR17
NFR2: Intent parsing and plan save should complete within reasonable time (<2 seconds)
Workflow follows BMAD pattern: load workflow.yaml, read instructions.md, execute steps in order

interface.name: Status Updater
interface.signature: Update status.yaml with mode, last_action, history

tests.standards: Manual verification workflow - run /sb:plan-one command, verify prompts, check output files. Unit tests embedded as subtasks (2.4, 3.4, 4.4, 6.5). Frontend Test Gate checklist provided in story with 11 test steps.

locations.location: Story file contains Frontend Test Gate with test steps table

ideas.idea: Run /plan-one with theme.json present → verify "Describe the slide you need" prompt appears
Describe "An architecture diagram showing 3-tier system" → verify summary shows purpose, elements, suggested template (layout-flow)
Describe "A bullet list of key features" → verify suggested template is layout-list
Describe "A Venn diagram showing concept overlap" → verify suggested template is "custom"
After confirmation, verify plan.yaml created with intent, suggested_template, key_points, created timestamp
After save, verify status.yaml shows mode: "single" and last_action: "Single slide planned"
Remove theme.json, run /plan-one → verify error "No theme found. Run /setup first."
